steps.title: Place however and the boundary
steps.content: The sentence needs to connect two independent clauses: the book wasn't written solely by Legler, and other authors also contributed. "However" comments on the first clause, and a semicolon can then separate the two clauses.

steps.title: Rule out punctuation errors
steps.content: A creates a comma splice. B and C leave "however" without the required comma when it starts the second clause. The version that sets off "however" after Legler and uses a semicolon before the next clause is grammatical. The answer is D.
